reviews.text:
Hubs has become a closet 3 wick candle and Wallflowers collector. True story.  Every-time I go away, I come back and find my stash mysteriously growing. Shhh. Don't tell him I told you! He's inspired me to come by here and pick him up treats every now and then... 

Coming to this location is really a pleasure, plus it's conveniently located in a shopping center I frequent often. The staff here is really amazing! Extremely friendly and helpful. Valerie who usually works during the week is outstanding, bubbly and full of life. You can tell she loves her job which is super refreshing! 

This store is always in tip-top shape, fully stocked at all times and very well organized

Yesterday we shopped as a family for their annual $8.50 3 wick candle sale. We stock up when they have these for our personal collection and to have on hand for quick hostess, secretary, mail lady gifts etc. They make a great "just a little something" gift. The place was insanely packed and yet this top-notch staff did a great job of keeping the 4 distinct lines moving, the smiles beaming and the dozens and dozens of customers from killing each other over pumpkin this, and twinkling that scented candles. 

They always hand out coupons for great sales as well as send emails. Make sure to give them your email address each and every visit to ensure the mail keeps coming. It's rare there's not a current coupon so no need to ever shop without getting a discount!
reviews.business_categories: Skin Care, Beauty & Spas, Gift Shops, Perfume, Flowers & Gifts, Shopping, Home & Garden, Day Spas, Cosmetics & Beauty Supply, Candle Stores

reviews.text:
I come here when I want to get a little creative with my caffeinated beverages. I'm a big fan of their "El Diablo", a raspberry mocha with cayenne. Definitely try it if you're a fan of a little pep! Most recently I tried the "Wooly Mammoth" which contained dark chocolate, cardamom, cinnamon and blackberry. It was very delicious! 

They always have two menu posters on the wall noting the seasonal drinks, both teas and coffee which they are currently offering as well as one poster with their signature house favorites. They are all uber creative and it's really hard each time I've visited to choose just one of the options with quirky names such as "Walking Dead" & "007".

Lucky Goat of course offers all of the standard coffee shop beverages, but honestly, that's not why you're going to want to come here. Stepping out of your comfort zone a bit here would be my advice. I love the feeling of coming in knowing I can try something new each and every time I visit, but can always go back to an old favorite if I so choose. 

There's a couch and some outdoor seating which I where you can usually find me. There are barstool seats by the side windows as well as two tops in the main room. There's also an upstairs which is a tad more spacious. There's games for adults and books for kiddos. 

I haven't tasted any of the food, but I do go a few blocks out of my way just for those unique drinks alone when in Fairmount/Brewery section of the city. I say try it! Oh, and they have almond and other non dairy milk varieties for the fellow veg-heads out there!
reviews.business_categories: Food, Coffee & Tea

reviews.text:
This is the original location of Primo's Hoagies! When you come here you can expect that old school South Philly hospitality. It's a tiny, no frills take out only corner shop selling really tasty hoagies (not subs for those out of towners). I actually think this particular location is the best out of all that we've tasted. 

What makes Primos hoagies so delicious is the perfectly crispy, toasted original Primo's Brand rolls. It's all in the roll if you ask me. 

If you're not sure what to order, you can't go wrong with a classic Italian. Hubs go-to is the 'Chicken Diablo' and I prefer 'Nonna's Veggie'. It's absolutely incredible! Breaded eggplant, roasted red peppers and broccoli rabe with sharp provolone. They will leave it off if you're dairy-free and I feel even then, it doesn't miss a beat. 

The sammies are reasonably priced and ready fairly quickly although there's definitely a steady lunch crowd around noon or so. Heading here at a slightly off time is your best bet especially for a legal parking spot also! Don't you dare block the street! 

Knocking off a star for their hours. They close at 4pm or whenever they run out of rolls. I can definitely appreciate them wanting to keep the rolls fresh but there have been many a times when we wanted to grab and easy dinner from here and it was impossible. That inconvenience is a total bummer. We would probably order from here a lot more often if their hours were more conducive to our schedule.
reviews.business_categories: Delis, Restaurants, Italian, Local Flavor, Sandwiches

reviews.text:
This spot was our jam back in the day when it was Kildares. On a tour of of old haunts in honor of my husband's recent birthday we decided to stop by despite being a bit super partial to the old residents. We were actually quite impressed with the renovations and the new vibes! 

The place is super hip and obviously inspired by music. I loved the photos of my old pals Janis and Jimi downstairs, and certainly the guitars and old school blues concert posters on the walls upstairs in the "juke joint". 

We came for date night and enjoyed live music by  Big Soul Chicken band. (Check their site for a listing of upcoming performers.)They were awesome and played their original tunes as well as classics we all know and love. Perfect place to come whether you want it as background music or more of a concert/dancing type of night. 

 While the "juke joint" wasn't nearly as nitty and gritty as the authentic ones I've been to on the back roads in Mississippi and in the south in general, it certainly gave me a bit of that area I desire from time to time. There's not enough live music places in the city in my opinion. Let alone free to enter ones! This place is a great find! 

It's super groovy, dark and sexy feeling up in the juke joint. Lots of candles and even a fireplace. Perfect for a night out with my love! 

The cocktails were awesome and ran about $26 for two before gratuity. We chose the Lynchburg Basil Lemonade (Jack Daniels, Fresh Basil, Homemade Southern Lemonade) and the Black Cherry Gimlet (Black Cherry Vodka, Vanilla, Lime, maraschino Cherry). I honestly cannot choose a favorite! Both were carefully crafted, alcohol filled and absolutely delicious! Whiskey and Bourbon are obviously their specialty and I really loved the barrels scattered about doubling as side tables! The beer list was rather extensive also! 

The menu, like the entire place, is influenced by the South. They have a late night menu, kids menu, brunch, lunch and dinner. We can't wait to return perhaps for brunch with the kids or "social hour" as they call it. There's daily specials during that 5-7pm period with $4 wines, $3 brews and $4-$6 smaller plates. The mushroom and kale salads were calling my name and the crawfish Mac and cheese sounds right up hub's alley. 

Super fun find!! 
Kiosk parking. Lots of spots. There's also an hourly lot under Abbots Square.
reviews.business_categories: Event Planning & Services, Whiskey Bars, American (New), American (Traditional), Venues & Event Spaces, Bars, Nightlife, Restaurants

reviews.text:
3.5 yelps Happy to have Benna's West in the 'hood as it's the only place for fresh pressed juices this side of Washington and west of Broad. This corner of the city desperately needs a place like this. I have a few gripes however... 

*Craving a green juice after the gym I stopped by Benna's which had caught my eye several times driving past. I had never been to the original but had heard nothing but good things. Expecting to choose a juice from a list of super cheesy names like "Green Machine", "Purple Passion" and there's always the inevitable, "Hangover Cure", all I see is a list of Ingredients. Confusion sank in for a moment... Now, I do like the idea of 'make your own juice' and I like the fact that I can add as many ingredients as I wish without a crazy up-charge and that they offer bell pepper which is somewhat of a rarity, but I am a regular juicer and know how to properly combine ingredients for a great tasting juice. Not everyone does. Not everything tastes, even looks good together. People who are newer to juices may feel a little overwhelmed with this method of ordering and may very well create something that tastes horrible. (I've done it in the early stages of juicing.) They may never drink juice or order juice here again. No one wants that. I think a few suggested combinations would be a rather smart addition to the menu for the juice newbies or even for someone that doesn't want to think it over too much. Leave the "make your own" as an option in addition to suggested combos. Was the juice even good you ask? Yea, I created it. 

* I had no idea that it was cash only. Yelpers, you failed me on this one. They do have an ATM on site with a $2.75 charge. This is definitely a pet peeve of mine. Not only is an ATM inside unsightly in an otherwise cute place, but what is it really to have a square reader or something similar in this day and age? Especially when you're already using a tablet for orders anyway and it's so simple to use! Up your prices a tad to cover the slight service fee. It's annoying!! This made my $5 juice a $7.75  which still isn't awful I just much rather use that money towards one of the delicious looking pastries that were staring at me as I ordered up my kale. 

* The sign on the door kind of rubbed me the wrong way. "Please leave bikes, strollers, dogs yada yada on the side street" or something to that effect. (See pic.) Just strange and bossy. I didn't however mind the "no smoking 20 feet from our front door" sign. Good call. Honestly though between the signage, the ATM and the not so friendly girls behind the counter, I wasn't feeling Benna's vibes which is a shame. 

Positives would be the location, brightly colored tables outside and healthy and clearly marked vegan options. The avocado grapefruit salad caught my eye as did the Kale salad with falafel. I unlike others didn't think the food prices were that steep. 

Will I be back? Sure. I would like to try the food. Plus, it's so close to home. I'll just remember to bring cash next time. 

*Lots of laptops going so if you're looking for a place to work, seems legit. 

*Free street parking right on Broad makes for and easy for a quick stop if driving.

3.5 yelps
reviews.business_categories: Juice Bars & Smoothies, Sandwiches, Food, Restaurants, Coffee & Tea

reviews.text:
It's a fine, basic nail salon and a good, cheap, quick option for when I'm in the area. I have never had any issues nor have I ever had to wait which is a nice change from the long waits in the smaller salons in the city where I live. Queen Spa is big, bright and clean. 

The man who always greets you as you walk in is very kind and seats you immediately. He'll jump in when need be if things get busy as well. 

My Mom has been coming here religiously for gel manis and pedis and had no complaints over the years. She used to see Nona who is now only there on the weekends, but she's a favorite among a few ladies I know so maybe give her a try. She's been there for years. It seems that choosing the older ladies who are more experienced is the way to go here. 

I have only gotten gel mani and pedi and they are a steal especially compared to the salons in the city! Monday through Friday they run a special, $35 for BOTH a gel mani and regular pedi. Craziness! 

Their regular pedicures are hot rock which I'm obsessed with! You have to pay extra in the city for this treatment. 

They have a cute special mouse (knock off Mickey) chair for the kiddo's to use which mine love! 

Designs are $5 extra 

Tips are cash only, but they do have an ATM on site which does NOT have a surcharge! 
 
Is it the best mani I've ever gotten? No 
Did I sip a glass of champagne while I was there? No 
Did my hubby love me for the price listed on the receipt? Yes.
reviews.business_categories: Nail Salons, Beauty & Spas, Massage

reviews.text:
The Comcast HD wall is one of the world's largest LED screens and quite frankly, pretty cool! Their holiday show is a part of our family's annual Philly Holiday tour! We've been coming here to view this awesome FREE show since the very first year it opened back in 2008 and we still enjoy it very much. We think you will too if haven't been already. 

The show itself hasn't changed much nor have the crowds diminished over the years, but we still come and cram in to watch the 15 minute show filled with everything you need to get you into the holiday spirit such as The Twelve Days of Christmas, dancing penguins, caroling, winter sleigh rides and my favorite part, The Nutcracker which is performed by The Pennsylvania Ballet. Oh, and the aerial scenes over Philly are really cool too! Make sure you stay to the end as there's some magical snow fall kiddos will love!

The holiday show runs from Thanksgiving to New Years at the top of each hour from 10am-8pm everyday except at 5pm on weekdays. 

We usually try to arrive early or make our way up front where parents and their wee ones usually grab a seat on the floor. Standing visitors stay towards the back. 

We usually visit here after a trip to Macy's, Rothman Rink and Christmas Village! We park once and walk to each location or Uber it to one and walk the rest. It all makes for a truly, lovely day in the city!
reviews.business_name: The Comcast Experience HD Video Wall
reviews.business_categories: Local Flavor

reviews.text:
Adorable little new Parisian bakery! 
Clean, bright, and oh so welcoming! 

With the bistro tables out front, the bicycle complete with a wire basket perfect for baguette carrying, and the selves shaped like The Eiffel Tower, I would say they carried out their concept quite flawlessly here. 

While they personally had me at just La Colombe, latte drafts, and a plethora of teas, their pastries glistening with butter, were mesmerizing. 

I stopped by way too quickly this visit (30 minute loading zone spot right out front) for a sweet treat to take home to my sweets. It was as chilly and raining and I couldn't help but wish I had more time and a good book with me because I just wanted to grab a table by the big windows up front and relax with some hot tea. I did NOT want to leave! 

Torn between one of the varieties of croissants, the chocolate bread pudding and the Philly Paris which looked like a custard filled eclair, I went with a heart shaped flaky pastry filled with beautifully and perfectly piped cream. It was said to be absolutely outrageous! Start to finish, no crumb left behind. It was a winner for sure! Thanks for making me look good you guys!

I can't wait to bring the whole fam here earlier in the day when the choices seem to be more plentiful, although it doesn't seem you can go wrong here either way. 

It's a great place to get some work done which many were doing, meet up with a friend and stay awhile, or grab a quick snack or caffeinated beverage to go! 

In addition to their freshly made baked goods which aren't just sweets, but loaves, quiche and sammies too, they also sell jams and other pre packaged goodies. 

Service was so friendly and the offer 10% discounts to Sweat members and Jefferson employees with badges. 

See you soon, J'aime (which is actually pronounced simply "Jem" like "...And The Holograms").
reviews.business_categories: Food, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Coffee & Tea, Restaurants, Bakeries, Cafes

reviews.text:
Loved Tuna Bar! Upon entering I was so impressed with the modern, trendy space and impeccable attention to detail every where I turned. So many decor pieces and architectural aspects I love here! They had me at wrap around banquet seating in a pop of turquoise. I had a feeling I was in for a good meal from the interior design alone. I was...

First visit was for a casual, lighter lunch with my daughters and an old girlfriend which it was just perfect for! We did make ressies on Open Table which wasn't necessary for a mid week lunch, but I would suggested for dinner. 

While we stuck to some basics, they were pretty great! California, California with real crab made crispy, cucumber roll, peanut avocado (the kids' favorite!), seaweed salad, edamame, and incredible Brussels! Must order! Everything was super fresh and came out in a timely fashion. 

Service was fantastic! 
Prices were fair. 

I can't wait for my next visit perhaps for date night when I can taste some of those delicious sounding cocktails and dig into some entrees. 

I love having Tuna Bar as a new option in the Old City area just a bit away from the hustle and bustle, whether that be for lunch, happy hour (which sounded really great!), or dinner...with the kiddos, or without. It will be be on my radar for the next First Friday or after a movie at The Ritz!

I actually found it a tad difficult to spot from the street. It's the door just to the left of United Blue. Not a huge sign. It's there, trust me :) 

*Limited kiosk meter parking.
reviews.business_categories: Sushi Bars, Restaurants, Japanese

reviews.text:
3.5 yelps. So it seems like our experience was not the norm and that's unfortunate for us. Seeing all of the other rave reviews makes me want to head back and see if the Sunday when we dined was just a rare fluke. I feel like we were jipped out of this amazing experience everyone else had. We were so excited to treat my parents to this meal. We were a tad embarrassed about how it turned out....

Right off the bat, ordering was fairly difficult. They did not speak one word of English. Many mistakes were made and any special requests we had were unable to be fulfilled because of the language barrier. We spoke with two individuals, neither understood us. At all. I think that unfortunately set the tone for the meal. 

We had a larger party and below is what everyone had to say. I personally can only comment on the mushroom tofu soup which I actually really loved and am totally craving right now after looking over my photos. The scallion pancakes were a tad soggy and lacking in flavor for me and the dumplings were just ok. I've had better. A tad too slippery and slimy for my taste. The plain rice was so delicious for some reason. Piping hot. Yum. 

3 yelps Beef soup. Not enough beef. Banchan Sides were good. Ribs were good. Chicken was just alright. 

3 yelps seafood tofu soup just two shrimp (one with eyeballs) Barely any seafood. "Floating yuck". Beef was really good. Chicken was just ok. "$12 for a bowl of tofu?"  Dumplings were good. A little mushy. 

2 yelps they don't have anything for kids. 

4 yelps Excellent rice plains and simple. 

3 yelps mushroom tofu soup was very good. Beef was the best out of everything. Chicken was average. 

3.5 yelps Chicken was not BBQ as stated. 
Scallion pancakes weren't crisp, were too greasy and a tad bland. 

Tons of food so think twice when ordering. Don't forget about all of the side that come first. The place was pretty cute. A nice find for the area. I think everyone could appreciate the authenticity of the meal for example the raw egg to place in our soon du bu, but Something just wasn't happening the way we had hoped and the way it seemed to for others.
reviews.business_categories: Korean, Restaurants

reviews.text:
I love supporting small businesses and I really love supporting local artists. I equally love giving unique gifts they won't easily find anywhere. Enter Philadelphia Independents. It's a great little small shop in Olde City with all the handmade Philly swag you can handle, designed by locals! 

I recently purchased a bada** Gritty card and one of our favorite local artist's sports tees for one of my husband 's Valentine's Day gift. He was thrilled and I felt great giving something so special that I know he would love and was just his style. 

They wrapped it for me and the owner was super sweet, introducing me to new designers and showing me all of her fun items. 

All around it was a fantastic stop, one I'll be making the next time I need a cool gift for a Philadelphian, even a new baby Philadelphian, as they have the cutest onesies! 

*It's also a great place to stop for a non-cheesy souvenir for visitors!

2 hr Kiosk meter parking.
reviews.business_categories: Gift Shops, Shopping, Arts & Entertainment, Flowers & Gifts

reviews.text:
Unfortunately I have frequented this place too often lately. So besides being pissed at myself for throwing money down the drain and being careless, I leave here completely satisfied and relieved. Super fast service. And the guy who owns the place is very nice and knowledgable. I had my screen fixed twice and my lcd screen fixed also. This place was cheaper than the 15 places I called in the city and could get me in and out a lot faster. The first time took about 30 minutes (just in time to check out the dollar store down the street) and the second time less than 2 hours because there were people ahead of me. I also then obviously bought a screen protector for a mere $20 which he so carefully applied for me. He cleaned it with a wipe, used special dusting tape and took his time applying it. My hubby who got his done at the Verizon store has bubbles. I would absolutely recommend this place and also recommend getting a good case. Duh. I had a fantastic one (a life proof case) until my daughter thought the lime green sealant rubber thingy was a necklace and stretched it out. No more expensive "jewelry" for my little girl.

**he also gave me great online marketing advice for my small business. They fix computers and do web design as well.
reviews.business_categories: Shopping, Computers, Specialty Schools, Marketing, Mobile Phone Repair, Professional Services, Telecommunications, Education, Graphic Design, Web Design, Mobile Phone Accessories, Home Network Installation, Security Systems, Legal Services, Local Services, Home Services, IT Services & Computer Repair

reviews.text:
I am constantly impressed with the services, honesty (and great conversation) provided by Dr. Cabrera. I'll say it once and I'll say it again, with any large practice, please find one doctor you love and stick with him or her! 

The office is lovely, uber clean, sleek and modern. Scheduling is available 24/7 and I have cancelled last minute with no charge. They also send text messages with appointment notifications with the option to confirm or cancel and receive an immediate call back to reschedule. So easy and efficient! 

Service from the receptionist out front offering waters, to the friendly and personable hygienist, to the billing guy when checking out has always been stellar. 

I loved my old dentist, but because of distance I had to switch. I was afraid I wouldn't find someone I like and trust as much, and I'm happy to say that wasn't the case when I found CCED! 

A visit here goes hand in hand with a shopping trip on Walnut. It makes heading to the dentist THAT less dreadful! 

Parking isn't impossible. It's kiosk meter, but I usually uber and stroll around afterwards with no rush or worry.
reviews.business_categories: Orthodontists, Health & Medical, Oral Surgeons, General Dentistry, Dentists, Cosmetic Dentists

reviews.text:
Potitos is a classic South Philly staple. While they do offer other assorted goodies as well as some creative towering cakes, you want to come here for the cannolis. You want to get the chocolate chip ricotta ("ri-got" is how you have to say it around here). And the answer is of course "yes" when asked if you want the powdered sugar on top. They're thick and creamy and rich. They can be cut in half as a half is all you really need, but you will want the whole thing. Hell, you'll want the whole box, dammit. 

Currently they're are $3.50 a piece. They have a annual sale on their anniversary where they sell them for the original price when they first opened. This is when you want to go for a large order, but this is also when you'll find a line all the way down Ritner Street. 

You want to be everyone's favorite at a party or dinner? Pick up a box. You're welcome. 

Parking is typical South Philly parking. It's rough. Real rough. Don't block the street as most do. It's usually a quick stop except before a big holiday. Just find a (semi) legal spot where people can still drive by.
reviews.business_categories: Food, Restaurants, Bakeries

reviews.text: I think the Black Sheep is my favorite in the city. They have yummy food (especially the fries), the first floor is lovely and bright when it's nice weather because of the big windows, and there are 3 floors so usually you can find a spot somewhere to have a drink.
reviews.business_categories: Bars, Pubs, Irish Pub, Nightlife, Sports Bars, Restaurants, American (New)

reviews.text: So far I've been to this friendly cafe twice and both times I've been transported to Paris. When you walk in you are greeted with a Bonjour and allowed to select from their delicious savory and sweet offerings like quiche Lorraine with onions and bacon or their pan au chocolat (our favorites so far). P. And I have gotten both their cappuccinos and regular coffee and both were on point. You're never made to feel rushed as you sit at a table and relax, which feels oh so Parisian. I will definitely be back to try some more of their baked goods, particularly their fruit tart. Also they have freshly baked baguettes and bread for sale, which look just right.
reviews.business_categories: Food, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Coffee & Tea, Restaurants, Bakeries, Cafes

reviews.text: My husband and I have bought a bunch of Shibe's sports related shirts over the past couple of years. The tshirts they use are great quality and soft and they always have unique designs that you know not everyone around you will have if you're planning on wearing something to a game. My MIL is a big flyers fan so we've gotten her a bunch of different flyers shirts and she loves them. Also the shopping bags they give you are reusable totes, which is a nice touch. I'm sure we'll be back the next time we're walking by and something new in the window looks interesting.
reviews.business_categories: Sporting Goods, Fashion, Souvenir Shops, Sports Wear, Shopping

reviews.text: This place is the old Paradigm/Dolce, so it's huge. We stopped in for an early dinner as we were seeing a show around the corner at a nearby theatre. For dinner we had the veggie pizza, yummy, thin crust with seasonal veggies (broccoli rabe, zucchini, and tomatoes) and the mussels in white sauce, very tasty! I kind of wish there wasn't any broccoli rabe on the pizza, though because I don't enjoy the bitter taste. Our waitress was very attentive we were in and out pretty quickly, which worked out for us since we literally had to run up the street to get to the theatre in time. Would recommend the bar as they seem to have a decent amount of specials and the food for a casual meal.
reviews.business_categories: Restaurants, Bars, Pizza, Nightlife, Italian

reviews.text: I thought bareburger was pretty tasty! We went in for take out thinking it was more like a five guys, but it actually looks like cool a place to sit down and have a burger and some fries. I got the standard because I'm boring and it was delish! The fries were nice and salty and the onion rings were good too. Id like to go back and try some of the other meats. The prices aren't cheap, but the location is so close to me, I can't complain. I'll be back.
reviews.business_categories: Restaurants, Burgers, American (New), Food, American (Traditional), Nightlife, Bars, Cocktail Bars, Salad

reviews.text: I really enjoyed my visit to Southgate last night. They were accommodating to our group of 7 (we got a big table towards the kitchen) and the food was great! We tried both styles of their Korean Fried Chicken (KFC) - the soy garlic and spicy gochujang. I really enjoyed both flavors. The spicy wasn't TOO spicy and the soy garlic was sweet and tangy. Both were a great compliment to the extra crispy crust on the chicken. For my entrée I went with the beef bibimbap and of course upgraded to the dolsot option ($2) extra which was worth getting the hot crispy rice. I ate the whole bowl and I'm not even mad. I tried 2 of their signature cocktails the "Korumbian" because the name just made me laugh and the Korea 75. The Korumbian was a little too sweet for my taste, but it was nice with the spicy wings and salty soy flavors of the dishes we got. I preferred the Korea 75 which is sparking wine with sake and a little citrus (grapefruit maybe?) I'm a sucker for anything with a little sparkle to it. I definitely would recommend this place for something that isn't your typical Italian or Chinese casual dining if you like interesting flavors. I will be back!
reviews.business_categories: Breakfast & Brunch, American (Traditional), Korean, Restaurants, Gastropubs, Nightlife, Bars, Cocktail Bars

reviews.text:
I went to Kanella with 3 of my friends before a girl's night out. My friend had heard good things about this restaurant and we were all excited to try it. I will say, I don't typically eat greek fare, so this type of food was a little different for me, which definitely may have affected my rating of this place.

For an appetizer, we shared the plate of mixed dips which included hummus, tzatziki (I think) and some other spread that the waiter told us was "cow manure" that I believe had some kind of fish in it. He was definitely trying to be funny and had a good personality, but I thought cow manure was a weird thing to call food that you were serving a restaurant. I don't think the chef's would have liked that too much haha. All the spreads were delicious though, even the "cow manure". 

Since we were going to be partying later and I had already stocked up on large amounts of the pita that was served and replenished often with our appetizer, I figured I would just have something lighter for dinner. I went with one of the soups of the day. I'm not really sure what it was called, but it had a lemon broth base with pulled pieces of chicken throughout mixed in with a curdled milk substance. I was not a big fan. It had a strange bitter taste that I am not  accustomed and they lemon mixed with the cream or milk was not to my liking. 

However, the other plates that my friends got were colorful and looked interesting, I just don't think authentic greek food is for me. Give me some spanakopita or a nice gyro and I'm set, but that's about as wild as I'll probably get.
reviews.business_categories: Mediterranean, Food, Greek, Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch

reviews.text: I've been to the Blue Duck a few times, including my bridal shower, and every time has been great! I've made it a point to try something different every time I've visited and I have yet to be disappointed. The pork roll burger is delicious and sriracha spicy and VERY messy so be prepared. The fried chicken sandwich is perfectly crispy. The House Grind is juicy and a safe bet for any burger lover out there. The General Tso wings are reminiscent of Korean BBQ wings and the General Tso chicken tacos that my friend had looked yummy too. The Blue Duck fries are a must. I repeat. You MUST get them. All the mac and cheeses are delightful, but I particularly enjoyed the green curry mac and cheese which have a hint of heat and curry flavor and are topped with crispy chickpeas and chicken. But you can't go wrong with any of their velveeta-esque shell mac and cheese. They are BYOB which is always fun when you're prepared and if you aren't they have lemonades, iced teas, and adorable mini glass coke bottles that you can pour into a mason jar. The staff here are so nice and patient, especially when we were there for a few different lunch parties with larger groups. The restaurant is decorated in a light country style which pairs perfectly with the mason jars and galvanized metal wine buckets they provide. I will definitely be returning! The Blue Jawn...I have my eye on you.
reviews.business_categories: Restaurants, American (New), Burgers, Breakfast & Brunch, Sandwiches

reviews.text: Paddy Whacks is a fun South Street bar for beers and mixed drinks. It's big so you never feel like your bumping elbows with people, which I like. Also, the Baby Guinness shots are so adorable. Haven't tried the food, but I would imagine you could get some decent pub fare here.
reviews.business_name: Paddy Whacks Irish Sports Pub - South Street
reviews.business_categories: Bars, Nightlife, Pubs

reviews.text: This a nice change of pace for the Free at Nooners, like myself, after taking in some fabulous, live XPN radio station music, which is right down the street. They have lots of seating for big groups (note: the Game of Thrones-Style giant wooden tables). They have a lot of beer on tap, especially Irish beers, and hefty portion sizes for their lunches. I got a filet medallion sandwich with onion rings and they definitely didn't skimp on the onion rings. Its a decent Irish pub in the area if you're feeling something different than Slainte or the World Cafe.
reviews.business_categories: Nightlife, Music Venues, Irish, Pubs, Irish Pub, Arts & Entertainment, Food, Restaurants, Breweries, Bars, Beer, Wine & Spirits

reviews.text: Saturday and Sunday brunch from 11-4! I'm a huge french fry fan and Black Sheep has some damn good fries. I would go just for the fries. And the beer of course. They definitely have a solid pub fare offering and I need to come back to try more things off their menu. I'll definitely return for brunch!
reviews.business_categories: Bars, Pubs, Irish Pub, Nightlife, Sports Bars, Restaurants, American (New)

reviews.text: This little shop is great for unique gifts for friends and family and even yourself. My husband bought a liberty bell and bolt tshirt and loved it so much we went back and bought him another one in a different color. He always gets compliments on it and it's so soft I can see why he wears it all the time. I've gotten baby onesies that have cute patterns for my friends kids too and I known they're not going to be like any other gift they'd get from anyone else. The prices are a little high, especially for pillows and housewares, but I've yet to find designs that rival theirs so I'll probably back for some Philly themed pillows sooner or later.
reviews.business_categories: Gift Shops, Shopping, Arts & Entertainment, Flowers & Gifts

reviews.text: This place is a nice, quirky addition to the area offering a huge assortment of beers that you can buy by the bottle and have with your lunch. The small menu includes hoagies with boars head meat, crispy Korean style chicken wings tossed in your choice of sauces, French fries topped with cheese, steak and fried onions if you're feeling frisky, cheeseburgers, and various specials like meatball sandwiches and fried chicken sandwiches. It's no frills and has a few tables, but it seems to work out fine as it seems to have a big take out presence, at least at lunchtime when I usually go. It's close to work and I love that I have the opportunity to try out new beer and cider options 1 at a time without having to invest in a pack or case before I know I like it. Chases Hop Shop is a great casual place to go for lunch when you're in the mood for delicious, double fried ginger soy wings with a side of cheesesteak fries washed down with a cold bottle of Crabbie's Ginger beer.
reviews.business_categories: Chicken Wings, Nightlife, Bars, Delis, Food, Beer Gardens, Sandwiches, Beer Bar, Restaurants, Beer, Wine & Spirits, Gastropubs

reviews.text: Best Chinese food in the city. The old city location is the original but this one's great too. Keep in mind that their "10" is HOT!! I loveeee spicy food and i could only do a 7....
reviews.business_categories: Restaurants, Chinese, Szechuan

reviews.text:
First Time Customer: free bikini line wax (perfect for right now!!!)

This place is super chic and cute. You check-in when you arrive and the individual rooms are super clean.  The technicians are professional and nice.  I had Stephanie and she is the sweetest girl ever. I will def be back to her. 

negative: they do a lot of pushing for their 'products' and 'deals' ... even after saying no they handed me a brochure with everything in ti... kind of annoying, but its ok b/c it didn't effect how my wax went! (thats why they get 4 not 5 stars)
reviews.business_categories: Beauty & Spas, Waxing, Hair Removal, Skin Care

reviews.text:
The perfect spot for: Date night, girls night, group night, whatever you want! 

I had been meaning to try Tuna Bar out since it opened up in the neighborhood, but I don't have a lot of friends that like sushi (shameful.... I know). My girlfriend and I decided to have girls night here and it was great! They have a nice selection of wines/cocktails and plenty of options for sushi. 

We ordered three rolls and shared between the two of us: the Wentz (special roll), the Fishtown (speciality roll), and a Spicy Salmon roll.  I think what I liked the most about this place was the plating. Everything is served on one long platter and perfectly set. We all know we 'eat with our eyes first' and this was definitely one of those nights. 

All three rolls were spectacular. Service was just ok, but honestly the food is great and it's a great addition to the neighborhood so I'll be back!
reviews.business_categories: Sushi Bars, Restaurants, Japanese

reviews.text:
Stopped by for HH before the Sixers game and I must say they have great HH deals. Certain appetizers are half off and their house wines are $4 a glass. The bar wasn't that busy as most people trickled in as the game began, but its a good space for friends to hang out and watch the game. 

The stuffed long hots were the favorite. They are spicy and stuffed to the max with perfectly cooked sausage. 

The flatbread on the other hand was meh. Its not on your usual flatbread. What i thought was going to be a naan or pita like texture was more like a cracker. It's so thin and the toppings are piled high which made the "cracker" soft and soggy. The taste of the veggies needed salt too. 

Last thing we ordered was the beet salad. Slices of tomato, beets, and mozzarella cheese with a balsamic vinegar drizzle. I didn't think i'd he a huge beet fan, but these were good. 

If you get a chance to stop by for HH definitely hit up their drink specials and get the stuffed long hots!
reviews.business_categories: Cocktail Bars, Bars, Italian, Nightlife, Restaurants

reviews.text: What a great little spot on South St. I never even knew this place existed! Their menu is simple (veggie, chicken, beef, tofu). Prices range from $7-$9 depending on the protein you choose.  I got a veggie bibimbap and it was delicious.  They give you plenty of rice and a whole assortment of veggies.  The fried egg is the star of course. The sauce is what makes the dish. It's spicy just the way i love it!
reviews.business_categories: Korean, Restaurants

reviews.text:
From the outside this place looks nice. Plenty of outdoor seating and umbrellas along with tall black gates. My friend and I decided to give it a shot. We were on our way back and decided to walk down South to grab something.  

The staff here are nice.  Saying hello as you walk in, walking you to your seat, and saying goodbye as you walk out. Attentive when your food comes out too. Comes back within 2-3 minutes to see if everything ok and if you need anything else at the moment.  

Drink choices are ok. Nothing too special.  I ordered the half rack ribs with a baked potato and broccoli (with garlic). I'd give the ribs a solid 6 out of 10. A little bit too chewy for my liking. Whole meal came to about $25. Not sure why... i mean the place was cool for dinner once, but I dont think i'll be back b/c nothing really stood out to me.
reviews.business_categories: Tex-Mex, Restaurants, American (Traditional), Nightlife, Bars

reviews.text:
Best Breakfast sandwich i have ever had (Breakfast Po Boy... $4.75). It is filing and the bread is so soft.  It's the best breakfast choice and it's one of their more popular items.  

I've also had their Jambalaya (chicken, sausage, and shrimp in it). Their cornbread is made fresh daily.  You really cant go wrong with this place.  I love it!
reviews.business_categories: Restaurants, Cajun/Creole

reviews.text:
My roommates and I were walking down Baltimore looking for a place to get something lite to eat/drink.  Thank God for Mood Café!!! This place deserves all 5 stars for its rating

We each got a Lassi: Mango, Fig, and Peach.  We tried each other's and let me tell you all three tasted DELICIOUS! I mean it's fresh ingredients and the lassi is made right in front of you there. None of this picking it from a fridge or something.  

The taste is the authentic Lassi taste.  Sweet depending on what flavor/variety you choose and tangy from the yogurt/milk combo.  I have made it my goal to try all 60 flavors that the owner has... and at $3.99(reg) and $4.99(combo) a cup why not? it's so affordable! 

Definitely will be making this place a regular weekly spot for me! :)
reviews.business_categories: Cafes, Pakistani, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Restaurants, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Halal, Indian, Coffee & Tea, Food

reviews.text:
After a long hiatus i'm back and happy to say Mood Café is as perfect as i remember. The guy who was working there last time is gone and the new gentleman Daniel is perfect. Daniel takes care of every customer as they walk in the door. He answers everyone's questions with a smile and your order is ready pretty quickly! Since my last visit, Mood Café has grown in lassi options. When i first started coming here there were 40 options. Now there are 140! Don't be afraid to ask for a tweak or change to a combo you think you'd like. Hassan/Daniel will do it for you! As always if you don't like it, they are more than willing to make you something else. Best customer service around. 

Hassan really cares about his customers. He thought i was so upset with my last visit (and Yelp update) i was never coming back again! FEAR NOT HASSAN. i'm here =) and i'll see you soon.
reviews.business_categories: Cafes, Pakistani, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Restaurants, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Halal, Indian, Coffee & Tea, Food

reviews.text:
listen... i get it... its McDonalds but come on! This place has the worst attention to detail i have ever had to deal with. I ordered a bacon egg n cheese biscuit with a coffee (2 creams hold the sugar). What i got? a bacon egg n cheese mcgriddle (ewwww) with  black coffee.... ugh

sucks that they dont get the orders right. so be warned CHECK YOUR ORDER before you drive off......
reviews.business_categories: Fast Food, Food, Restaurants, Coffee & Tea, Burgers

reviews.text:
Had a $25 gift card for this BYO spot so we decided to give it a try. Purchased the guacamole appetizer which was a small portion. It tasted nice, but i wished it was bigger in size. The appetizer nachos with chicken were the nights hit. These bad boys were piled mile high and loaded with cheese, beans, sour cream and guac. I could have just eaten the nachos, but being the true fatty I am we all ordered tacos too lol. The table had two orders of the al pastor tacos and I got the shrimp tacos. Everyone shared. Overall the tacos were a solid 3.5/5. My only complaint was that the shells were too soft so the tacos kept falling apart. 

I do love that this is a BYO spot. It adds to its charm. Most people order to-go orders. During our time here there was only one other table sitting inside.
reviews.business_categories: Food, Tacos, Mexican, Latin American, Restaurants, Spanish

reviews.text: New guy on payroll is rude and slow. I wish Hassan was there when i went bc i would have pulled him over to the side to mention it. Kid needs to get his act together or find a new job.
reviews.business_categories: Cafes, Pakistani, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Restaurants, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Halal, Indian, Coffee & Tea, Food

reviews.text:
This bar had been on my to-do list for quite some time and I'm happy i finally made the trip. The first thing i noticed when i walked in is the decor is all mismatched. It's different and i get its the look they are going for so i didn't mind it. it was unique and i like finding different places like this in the city. we were told to sit wherever we want and so we made our way to the front couch. I like to people watch so this was a great view of the entire bar. 

Our first drinks were prepared and brought to the table timely. I must say there was only one waitress working the floor and i could tell she was getting nervous because the place was starting to get crowded. Our second drinks took forever to come out. I don't know if she forgot, or that they were ready, sitting at the bar, and she didn't get to them fast enough, but it sucked that it took forever. 

i liked the atmosphere, and when i did get my drinks they were tasty, but i hope management understands that one person can't handle an entire floor on his/her own. Service would improve substantially with just one more person working.
reviews.business_categories: Bars, Nightlife, Lounges, Cocktail Bars

reviews.text:
We came here for dinner because there wasn't really any other option for us; there isn't a lot in the way of nice restaurants within walking distance to World Cafe Live. The happy hour specials are good - lots of $5 apps, of which we got three. The hummus platter was great, but I wish they would give you more olives and forget the diced tomatoes. The pita bread was really delicious. The spinach and artichoke dip was good, but you could tell from one bite that it was loaded with mayo and cream cheese or heavy cream or just generally fatty stuff. I mean, it was thick, thick, thick. Rich. Intense. Borderline overwhelming.

The rest of the food was just OK. My chicken tenders were pretty blah and the French fries were edible but nothing to get excited about.

I wouldn't make any effort to go back, but if you're in a pinch and need somewhere to eat within walking distance to WCL, it's a decent choice.
reviews.business_categories: Nightlife, Bars, Restaurants, Irish, Pubs

reviews.text:
I think my eyelid is still twitching from my experience with Comcast.

I've wasted HOURS of my life the past week dealing with Comcast in person and over the phone. They have been none too helpful, but this center was absolutely the worst of my experience. Honestly I would have rather gone to the DMV than spent 40 minutes in line at this joint just to have the agent be a complete and total biatch who couldn't (WOULDN'T) help me at all.

To top it off, the line was over 16 people long and it was hot as hell.

STAY AWAY!!!!!
reviews.business_categories: Security Systems, Internet Service Providers, Home Services, Television Service Providers, Professional Services

reviews.text: I come here JUST for the mac and cheese. It's the best I've had in the city. Seriously. I don't know what's in it or how they do it, but it's out of this world. We recently went for brunch and it wasn't on the brunch menu, but the waitress said that was just a mistake and they do serve it for brunch/lunch. Don't hesitate to ask! Anyway, their other food is also good, and they've got plenty to drink, but it's all about that cheesy macaroni for me. They also have a fireplace, which is super cool when it's chilly out. There's lots of seating, if that's something you're worried about. Definitely try this place at least once!
reviews.business_categories: Bars, Pubs, Irish Pub, Nightlife, Sports Bars, Restaurants, American (New)

reviews.text:
My husband and I came to Jon's because we were going to see a movie and needed to get something to eat first. It was blazing hot out, so we wanted to go somewhere with air conditioning where there wouldn't be a wait. Jon's met our needs.

I have to say, I was really surprised by our meal. I got the southwest panini and he got the veggie panini. We each ate half of the other's sandwich, and both panini's were declicious. Like, surprisingly good. The southwest one came with this spicy mayo that I couldn't get enough of. The portion size was PERFECT, which has recently become important to me. We each finished everything on our plates and didn't leave feeling disgustingly stuffed.

On another note, we opted for salads instead of fries or chips, mostly because there were about 15 dressing choices and several piqued my interest. I got the Asian ginger, and it was one of the best dressings I've ever had. He got the pineapple mango, which was decent, but didn't compare to mine.

The one thing that really disappointed me, aside from the unsually loud atmosphere, was the drinks. I got a peach margarita and it tasted just like peach schnapps. It didn't seem fresh or natural at all. I then opted for a raspberry margarita, and it tasted like grape soda. Yuck. Totally not worth the $9.50.

I won't be making a huge effort to return here, but if I find myself in the area needing a place to grab a quick bite, it's definitely a good option!
reviews.business_categories: Tex-Mex, Restaurants, American (Traditional), Nightlife, Bars

reviews.text:
FINALLY!

This salon has everything that I want (except a debit/credit card machine)...

1. It's in my neighborhood and there's plenty of parking around.

2. The decor is welcoming and rustic. You can tell they're not trying too hard (like Salon Blush) but it's also not grimy or under-decorated (Shampoo Bar Salon & Spa).

3. They use natural products that don't smell toxic or abrasive. 

4. They let people bring their doggies inside. There were two dogs when I was there, and they were just running around being playful and entertaining.

5. They open at 9 am on Saturdays. With so many people having Saturday weddings that start in the afternoon it can be really hard to secure a 10:00 hair appointment just to have it last an hour and then have to rush home and do your makeup and get dressed before hitting the road to get to the wedding. The 9:00 open-time is a real blessing.

Now onto brass tacks... I saw Bridget for an updo for a wedding and couldn't have been happier. She was super friendly and tried very hard to accommodate me. She stopped what she was doing several times to have me check the progress and point out what I liked or didn't like. When she was done and I was doing my final check she told me that if I didn't like it she would "take it all out and start over." That wasn't necessary, but it was really nice to have someone actually care more about whether their client liked the product than to just churn people out as fast as possible.

The cherry on top is that all of the ladies that work there are super cute! It's always nice to go get your hair done by someone that actually looks good themselves. 

The next time I'm due for a haircut I'm going to try them out and see if I am still as happy. I suspect I will be!
reviews.business_categories: Waxing, Beauty & Spas, Eyelash Service, Used, Vintage & Consignment, Hair Salons, Hair Removal, Makeup Artists, Shopping, Fashion

reviews.text: This store is very cute, but it's also expensive. If you're looking for a really unique wedding or bridal shower card, or if you want to get a thoughtful gift of stationary for a friend, this is the place to be. Just be prepared to drop a couple more bucks than you typically would at CVS or Target for the same type of item.
reviews.business_categories: Bridal, Cards & Stationery, Office Equipment, Shopping, Arts & Crafts, Event Planning & Services, Home & Garden, Flowers & Gifts, Home Decor, Gift Shops

reviews.text:
Ambiance: 5 stars
Food: 4 stars
Service: 2 stars
Price: 2 stars

I had the chance to try Tuna Bar for the first time with my husband and my cousin. We were all really impressed with the decor and atmosphere of the restaurant (even if we did get blasted with cold air every time someone new entered).

We started with the pork gyoza and fried oysters. When we asked our server how many oysters came in an order, she said 2 per order. We ordered two so that all of us could try an oyster. When the plates came out, there were three gigantic oysters on each of them. So, we really didn't need to order 2 at all. The oysters were OK, nothing special. They mostly tasted like breading. The gyoza were disappointing. They really didn't have much flavor and tasted basically like plain ol' pork sausage.

The actual sushi itself was really good, though. I got some salmon sushi, a yellowtail and chive roll with scallion oil, and a spicy tuna roll. I loved the sushi - it was so tender and melted in your mouth. I also can't say enough good things about the yellowtail/chive/scallion roll. It was...really unique and delicious. I would definitely order that again and advise that you try it for yourself!

Overall, I enjoyed my meal here. I thought their cocktail list left a lot to be desired and that the price for everything was too steep for what you actually got. While I won't be making any grand effort to return, I wasn't dissatisfied with my meal here.
reviews.business_categories: Sushi Bars, Restaurants, Japanese

reviews.text:
This was my first real steakhouse experience, and I was not impressed. It's possible that I just am not a fan of steakhouses, or that I just don't *get* what all the fuss is about, but I left feeling monetarily violated.

I came here with my husband and his boss for a dinner, and I arrived about 20 minutes before they did. I went to the bar to get a drink. It was $16.00. For one drink. Without tip. We are not in NYC, folks. We are in Philadelphia, where every other bar or restaurant has specialty cocktails that are delicious and obtainable for $10.00 or less.

While sitting at the bar drinking my expensive drink, I took in the ambiance of this place. It was not appealing to me. It seemed dated, dark, and uninviting.

The appetizer I had was just OK. I got the "five onion" soup, which had barely any broth in it at all. It was basically a pile of onions covered with cheese. I've had a million French onion soups in my life, and this one was just not great.

Our dinner was fine. Just fine. I got the filet oscar, which was decent. My husband got the rib eye. It was also good. However, my husband could have (and does) make much, much tastier steaks on the reg. I don't need to spend $50.00 on ONE 6 oz piece of steak, when I could make it at home for much less. 

I didn't like our side of asparagus, either. It was so thick. I am sure some people like that, but it was literally over a half an inch thick, and I hate it. I prefer skinnier asparagus, as the fat kind is very difficult to cook the whole way though. So, I didn't really have any veggies to go with my steak.

The server we had and my bartender were both very nice and attentive, but I just don't see any way in hell that our meal was worth $400.00. The experience wasn't spectacular, the food didn't blow me away, and I left feeling guilty that my husband's boss had just spent so much money on something so underwhelming.
reviews.business_categories: Steakhouses, Restaurants, Nightlife, Seafood, Bars, American (Traditional)

The store is big, clean, and they've got tons of Skinnygirl drinks, even the brand new releases. I asked where to find an item and was promptly walked over to the appropriate section by a helpful employee.

And, of course, who doesn't like a big, free, parking lot just steps away?

reviews.text:
I was having some health issues back in August and since I'm a Temple employee and all my co-pays are waived if I visit the TU Health System for my needs, I wanted to make an appointment with a Temple doc. I called to the Health Science campus and was unable to get an appointment for a month. I scheduled an appointment, but when my symptoms began to escalate I contacted the Comprehensive Health Center on the Episcopal campus to see if I could get a sooner appointment. I had previously been to the Episcopal campus to see a therapist (I was getting married/having stressors in my life! Everyone needs someone to chat with, right?) and it was an OK experience. I later decided to cope on my own. Anyway, I thought seeing a family doctor at Episcopal would be a viable option since they're close and I had a decent experience with the therapist.

BOY, was I WRONG!!!!!!! My appointment was for 4:30 on a Wednesday. I arrived at 4:15 and stood in line for 20 minutes before I was even greeted by a receptionist. There was only one working the window, even though I saw three other people sitting at computers behind her talking to each other and on the phone. Even though the receptionist was working with a difficult patient and the line was forming behind her, no one thought it would be a good idea to assist. Anyway, at 4:35 I "signed in" and filled out a form. Then the waiting began.............I waited until 5:30 to even be called back to a private room. The room was a disaster. There was writing all over the walls, on their signs, and the general feel of the room was GRIMEY/FILTHY/GROSS. I was really fed up by 5:30, but I was feeling pretty crappy and I figured I had waited long enough that I might as well keep waiting. Anyway, I waited. And waited. And waited. And by 6:10 I decided to get the f outta there. As I left the nurse said that the doctor (Dr. Eisenberg) was "on his way," as she had told me numerous times before. 

Bottom line: Go anywhere else. ANYWHERE. Because you're not going to be served here.

I eventually made an appointment at Temple Palmer Park. The physican was very nice (Dr. Thatchet), responsive to my follow-up phone call, and the place was CLEAN.
reviews.business_categories: Hospitals, Health & Medical

reviews.text:
I have had two experiences with TU Ophthalmology. The first was in July of 2010 when I had a month long migraine. I was trying to find any possible cause or solution and went to a variety of doctors (ENTs, neurologists, accupuncturists, chiropractors, etc.). I had never had a real eye exam before so I called up Temple Ophthalmology and got an appointment right away (within a few days).

While the waiting room was very crowded and I did need to wait for a little while after my actual appointment, when I saw Dr. Domoraski I was very pleased. Since I had never had an eye exam before I was a little nervous, but it was completely painless and he explained everything he was doing as he was doing it. It turns out that I have 20/10 vision and my eyes were not the culprit of my migraines. None the less, the experience was quite positive.

My second experience was this past Monday. My husband has been having headaches and ended up staying home from work. I have been telling him that he needs to get his vision checked (he has glasses and contacts) and he's been procrastinating. So, I called up the TU Hospital and asked if I could make an appointment for him. The woman I spoke with was VERY nice and even though I was calling at 8 a.m. she got him an appointment for 11:45 that day with Dr. Nguyen! He was very happy with his appointment and said that Dr. Nguyen told him that his glasses and contacts are actually TOO STRONG and that could be causing some of his headaches. They also spoke about his activities (how much reading he does, how much time he spends at his computer, how much driving he does, etc.) and she explained that some of his activities and how he functions could also be part of the problem.

He now needs to make a follow up appointment for a contact fitting, but I am sure that everything will go well. Yay for Temple Ophthalmology.
reviews.business_categories: Hospitals, Health & Medical, Optometrists
